operation: Start with a default XY workplane. First, generate a 10 x 10 x 10 unit box that is centered along the X and Y axes, but not centered along the Z axis so its bottom face rests flush with the initial workplane, then tag this base box as 'brim'. Next, select the top Z face of the base box and create a new workplane aligned to this face. Finally, create a 5 x 5 x 5 unit box on this upper workplane, centered along the X and Y axes of the workplane and not centered along Z, so it sits fully on the top surface of the lower box and extrudes upward.
geometry: This is a two-tiered stacked cubic structure consisting of two square prisms. A larger square base prism supports a smaller square prism that is perfectly concentrically aligned on the top face of the base, sharing the same central vertical axis. All faces are planar and meet at sharp right angles, with the cross-section of the part reducing to a smaller square in its upper section.
function: This is a basic stepped test part intended for calibrating dimensional accuracy of 3D printers or CNC machining equipment, or acting as a small concentric standoff for low-load mechanical assembly use cases.
